articles.title: NASA Rolls Out Artemis III Moon Rocket Core Stage
articles.summary: Following the recent successful test flight of NASA’s Artemis II mission around the Moon, NASA rolled out the core stage, or the largest section, of the agency’s SLS (Space Launch System) rocket that will launch the crewed Artemis III mission in 2027. The stage departed from the agency’s Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans on […]

articles.title: NASA Invites Media to SpaceX’s 34th Resupply Launch to Space Station
articles.summary: Media accreditation is open for the next U.S. launch to deliver NASA science investigations, supplies, and equipment to the International Space Station. This launch is the 34th SpaceX Commercial Resupply Services mission to the orbital laboratory for NASA and will lift off on the company’s Falcon 9 rocket. NASA and SpaceX are targeting no earlier […]

articles.title: NASA Welcomes Latvia as Newest Artemis Accords Signatory
articles.summary: The Republic of Latvia signed the Artemis Accords Monday during a ceremony hosted by NASA at the agency’s headquarters in Washington, becoming the 62nd nation to commit to responsible space exploration for all humanity.  “We are proud to welcome Latvia to the Artemis Accords,” said NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman. “Each new signatory strengthens a coalition committed to the transparent and peaceful exploration of space. The accords are the […]

articles.title: Wheels Up for X-59
articles.summary: NASA’s X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft flies over the Mojave Desert in California in this April 14, 2026, image. The transition to flying with wheels up is a key milestone and an important step in the experimental aircraft’s test campaign. The X-59 has made its highest and fastest flights so far, expanding its operational range […]

articles.title: NASA’s Hubble Dazzles With Young Stars in Trifid Nebula
articles.summary: This shimmering region of star-formation, a close-up of the Trifid Nebula about 5,000 light-years from Earth, was captured in intricate detail by NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope. The colors in Hubble’s visible light image, which marks the 36th anniversary of the mission’s launch on April 24, are reminiscent of an underwater scene filled with fine-grained sediments fluttering through the ocean’s depths.

articles.title: Thailand’s Krabi Coast
articles.summary: The coastal province features striking tropical karst landscapes and sandy beaches alongside a mix of natural land cover and developed areas.

articles.title: CSDA Quality Assessment Report Evaluates Satellogic NewSat Data
articles.summary: The report adds to the growing documentation on commercial data’s contributions to Earth science research and applications.

articles.summary: Join us April 29 at 2:00 p.m. EDT to learn more about CSDA program vendor MDA Space.

articles.title: NASA Artemis II Human Research Data Methodology Challenge
articles.summary: NASA’s Human Research Program (HRP) uses research to develop methods to protect the health and performance of astronauts in space. In support of NASA’s goals for long-term missions on the surface of the Moon and human exploration of Mars, HRP is using ground research facilities, the International Space Station, and analog environments to monitor human health in deep space.  NASA’s Artemis II mission was the first […]
